title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington phase out gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
73% of over 250 respondents oppose the bans, citing practicality and economic impact.
Supporters of the bans highlight issues like noise, fumes, and health impacts on communities.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The new COVID variant BA.3.2, nicknamed 'Cicada', was detected in Massachusetts wastewater, originating from South Africa in 2024.
BA.3.2 evades antibodies from 2025-2026 vaccines, raising concerns about vaccine efficacy and potential summer surges.
The World Health Organization reports BA.3.2 has not increased hospitalizations, and vaccines still prevent severe disease.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled that DCF employees Katheryn Butterfield and Candice Gemski could stand trial for child seizure in Waltham.
The lawsuit claims DCF removed children without consent or evidence of abuse, violating the family's constitutional rights.
Judge Patti Saris dismissed other defendants, but noted a jury could find Butterfield and Gemski liable for ordering the seizure.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Three New England restaurants are named among Food & Wine's 12 most anticipated spring openings nationwide.
Willie's on Beacon Hill by Jamie Bissonnette offers pizzas and global-inspired pastas at 20 Charles St., Boston.
Ladyfish in Portland and La Tavernetta in East Boston are also among the anticipated openings this season.

title: The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Kate Quinn's latest novel, 'The Astral Library,' features the Boston Public Library as a magical portal.
'The Astral Library,' a pivot to fantasy for Quinn, debuted at No. 2 on the NYT bestseller list.
Quinn, a Boston University alum, spent time writing at the BPL during her studies in voice performance.

title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston councilors push for police bodycam footage release after a deadly Roxbury shooting involving Officer Nicholas O'Malley.
Resolution passed encouraging oversight of police video access; it had 9 votes in favor, 3 against, and 1 present.
DA Kevin Hayden cites ongoing investigation for withholding footage; Councilors and Ayanna Pressley demand transparency.

title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Quantum Accelerator, New England's only dual-launch straddle coaster, opens April 11 at Six Flags New England.
The coaster features a 2-minute ride with two launches, reaching speeds of 30 and 45 mph.
Quantum Accelerator is Six Flags New England's twelfth coaster, adding to over 100 attractions in the 200-acre park.

title: A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Public Schools will implement an AI educational program starting September, targeting AI proficiency for all high school graduates.
The initiative is supported by a $1 million grant from Kayak co-founder Paul English, a Boston Public Schools alum.
The curriculum focuses on ethical AI use and will offer teacher training and courses developed with UMass Boston.

title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's lawyers amended a court filing, clarifying his brother is not the father of Sandra Birchmore's unborn child.
The amended filing was issued after a reporter misinterpreted a footnote, spreading incorrect information about William Farwell.
Matthew Farwell, held in a Rhode Island detention center, awaits trial for Birchmore's murder, seeking release on a $50,000 bail.

title: A Boston student runs the Marathon to honor her best friend — and the father she lost
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lily Penzone, a 21-year-old Boston student, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon honoring her best friend's late father.
Penzone supports trauma research by raising money for Brigham and Women's Stepping Strong team during the marathon.
The marathon holds personal significance as Abby's father, whom Penzone honors, ran multiple Boston Marathons.
